sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_structs (id INTEGER, data STRUCT<value1 INTEGER, value2 INTEGER>);
INSERT INTO test_structs VALUES (1, row(100, 200)), (2, row(300, 400));
SELECT struct[data][1] + INTERVAL 1 HOUR AS plus_one_hour FROM test_structs;
SELECT struct[data][2] + INTERVAL 2 DAY AS plus_two_days, id FROM (SELECT id, data FROM test_structs WHERE struct[data][1] > 150) AS filtered;
CREATE TABLE test_dates (date_col DATE, time_col TIME);
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (DATE '2023-01-01', TIME '10:00:00'), (DATE '2023-02-01', TIME '15:30:00');
SELECT (SELECT struct[data][1] FROM test_structs WHERE id = 1) + (SELECT time_col FROM test_dates WHERE date_col = DATE '2023-01-01') AS combined_time;
SELECT id, struct[data][1] + INTERVAL struct[data][2] HOUR AS mixed_interval FROM test_structs WHERE struct[data][1] IS NOT NULL;
SELECT struct[data][1] + INTERVAL (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_structs) HOUR AS nested_count_hour FROM test_structs GROUP BY struct[data][1];
CREATE TABLE test_mixed (str_col VARCHAR, num_col INTEGER);
SELECT str_col, struct[data][1] + INTERVAL CAST(num_col AS INTEGER) HOUR AS plus_boundary FROM test_structs JOIN test_mixed ON struct[data][1] = num_col WHERE str_col IS NOT NULL;
SELECT struct[data][1] + INTERVAL 1 DAY AS daily_increment, id FROM (SELECT * FROM test_structs WHERE struct[data][2] < 300) AS tmp HAVING daily_increment IS NOT NULL;
SELECT struct[data][1] + INTERVAL 1 HOUR AS incremented_time FROM (SELECT struct[data][1], struct[data][2] FROM test_structs WHERE struct[data][1] IS NOT NULL) AS derived WHERE struct[data][2] > 200;
